search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae type 2 diabetes annual review telehealth Issue 2 Dubai
site:diabetesjournals.org 2026 comprehensive diabetes medical evaluation annual follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diabetes control conclusion or complication diagnosis
automatic medicine, dose, target, screening order or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Annual review documents clinician-assessed status and does not itself establish control or complications.
All treatment and screening decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: DHA evidence is adult type 2 diabetes; ADA includes broader diabetes populations with recommendation-specific qualifiers. Children, pregnancy and type 1 diabetes require additional review.
setting_applicability: Outpatient annual diabetes review; DHA evidence is telehealth-specific.
UAE_applicability: DHA is UAE-direct for Dubai telehealth; UAE facility laboratory, screening, medicine and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 and ADA Standards 2026 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current registered official versions were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA annual-review section supports interval change, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, measured vital signs and weight or BMI, foot or skin assessment, laboratory and home-monitoring review for adults with type 2 diabetes in Dubai telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ADA evaluation section supports patient goals, functional impact, focused assessment, previous correspondence, clinician plan, safety discussion and continuing follow-up without generating management.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic status, current treatment, complications, comorbidities, overall and functional status, support, risk factors, shared goals and continuing-care plans.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Metabolic-decompensation and foot red flags were reviewed as prompts only; no condition or escalation is inferred.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

unresolved_source_gaps: Diabetes type, duration, exact medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring data, HbA1c, BP, BMI, renal, lipid, retinal, foot, vaccination, complication, diagnosis and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
